instructions.instruction: Check REVIEWED.md and references/REFERENCE_INDEX.md for duplicates before creating any file
instructions.original_snippets: Search REVIEWED.md and references/REFERENCE_INDEX.md for the slug or arxiv ID. If already present, report it and stop.
instructions.relevant_when: User provides an arxiv ID, arxiv URL, DOI, or paper PDF to triage

instructions.instruction: Derive a stable slug using <firstauthor-surname>-<2-3-word-topic> convention
instructions.original_snippets: Derive a stable slug: <firstauthor-surname>-<2-3-word-topic> (e.g. jiang-llmlingua, press-longchat).
instructions.relevant_when: Before creating any file for a new paper

instructions.instruction: Create references/<slug>.md using templates/REFERENCE-paper.md and populate every section
instructions.original_snippets: Create references/<slug>.md using templates/REFERENCE-paper.md. Populate every section: TL;DR, What's novel, Mechanism overview, Evaluation, Implementation details worth stealing, Open questions.
instructions.relevant_when: After duplicate check passes and slug is derived

instructions.original_snippets: TL;DR: 3–8 bullets capturing what the paper does and why it matters.

instructions.instruction: Always mark benchmark metrics and evaluation results as (as reported) — never state them as verified facts
instructions.original_snippets: Evaluation: quote benchmark name, baselines, key metric, and result — always mark (as reported).
instructions.relevant_when: Filling in the Evaluation section of the reference summary

instructions.instruction: Add a reverse-chronological row to REVIEWED.md summary table and a detailed section below it
instructions.original_snippets: Add a row to the summary table at the top (reverse-chronological)... Add a detailed section below the table.

instructions.instruction: Add a row to REFERENCE_INDEX.md under the most relevant category
instructions.original_snippets: Add a row under the most relevant category table. If no category fits, add it to the closest one and note it for the user.

instructions.instruction: Never create ANALYSIS-*.md automatically — offer promotion as an option and wait for user confirmation
instructions.original_snippets: Triage-then-promote: every paper enters via REVIEWED.md; promotion to ANALYSIS-*.md requires a deliberate user decision — never automatic.
instructions.relevant_when: At the end of every triage workflow

instructions.instruction: Skip sections not applicable to the paper and mark them N/A rather than leaving them blank
instructions.original_snippets: Skip sections that are not applicable (mark N/A).
instructions.relevant_when: Filling in the Mechanism overview section

instructions.instruction: Run validate-reference-paper.sh on the completed file before reporting
